title: The Convergence Before Divergence

features.name: river-memory-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water that hasn't learned to be many channels yet, still remembering being singular. The pool holds the convergence: every drop knows where it came from.

features.name: confluence-marker
features.desc-on-touch: Where three water-paths learned to be one. The stone here is smoothed by the logic of meeting, polished by pressure toward union.

features.name: language-sediment
features.desc-on-touch: Where the archive recorded water-sounds, it caught something: words layered in the sediment, from before the rivers learned to be separate dialects.

features.name: flow-decision-rock
features.desc-on-touch: The water splits here, or comes together—the tense is uncertain. The rock remembers both states happening at once, pressure and release simultaneous.

features.name: echo-of-singular
features.desc-on-touch: The water remembers being undivided. It hums in a single pitch here, before the channels teach it harmony and discord.

features.name: splitting-observation-shelf
features.desc-on-touch: A ledge where someone stood to watch the moment of divergence. The archive found their notes: which one is leaving, which one is staying? Never answered.

inscription: the archive keeps the union and the fracture indivisible; choice flows downstream

file.name: RiverSource_Hydrology_Annotated_Bilingual_Split.txt
file.note: Hydrological records of river confluence written in two languages that gradually diverge. Later entries contradict earlier ones about which tributary flows where, as if the rivers rerouted themselves

exits.label: back up to the echo-field where repetition began
downstream where the split channels remember being one
west to where tributary memory pools before joining
